summary:
• Russian refinery and pumping station strikes by Ukraine are creating significant supply disruptions that contribute to widening global oil price premiums and Western Hemisphere cost pressures.

• A net crude supply loss of 9 million barrels per day persists despite increased Atlantic exports, indicating severe geopolitical constraints on global supply availability are supporting higher prices across Americas markets.

• Middle East escalation and Iran tensions are accelerating supply concerns that could further tighten Western Hemisphere crude availability, with California already experiencing $6+ gasoline as a harbinger of broader regional price volatility.
